revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社 |名称 = 御山神社 |画像 = [[File:Miyama Jinjya(Hiroshima).JPG|280px]] |所在地 = 広島県廿日市市宮島町 | 緯度度 =34 |緯度...'
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 御山神社 |画像 = [[File:Miyama Jinjya(Hiroshima).JPG|280px]] |所在地 = 広島県廿日市市宮島町 | 緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =16 |緯度秒 =39.62 | 経度度 =132 |経度分 =19 |経度秒 =6.71 |祭神 = 市杵島姫命、田心姫命、湍津姫命 |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = 一間社流造 |別名 = さんきさん |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''御山神社'''（みやまじんじゃ）は[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]の[[厳島|厳島（宮島）]]にある[[神社]]。 [[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の山頂付近にあり、[[厳島神社]]の奥宮である。
==祭神== 厳島神社同様、[[宗像三女神]]を祀る。 *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと）
==歴史== [[平清盛]]が厳島神社を造営した際、奥宮として建立したと伝えられる。</br> [[江戸時代]]までは[[三鬼大権現|三鬼神]]（追帳鬼神・魔羅鬼神・時眉鬼神）を祀る三鬼堂であったが、 [[神仏分離令]]により宗像三女神を祀る御山神社となり、三鬼堂は[[弥山本堂]]近くに新たに建立された。
==社殿・境内== 三女神を祀る[[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿三棟が品字形に並ぶ。
==交通アクセス== * [[広島観光開発|宮島ロープウェー]]獅子岩駅から、弥山本堂経由で約30分。
==関連項目== *[[厳島神社]] *[[弥山 (広島県)]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:厳島神社]] [[Category:厳島 (広島県)]]

{{神社 |名称= 御山神社 |画像= [[File:Miyama Jinjya(Hiroshima).JPG|270px]] |所在地= 広島県廿日市市宮島町 |緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =16 |緯度秒 =39.62 |経度度 =132 |経度分 =19 |経度秒 =6.71 |地図= Japan Hiroshima |祭神= 市杵島姫命、田心姫命、湍津姫命 |社格= |創建= |本殿= 一間社流造 |別名= さんきさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''御山神社'''（みやまじんじゃ）は[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]の[[厳島|厳島（宮島）]]にある[[神社]]。 [[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の山頂付近にあり、[[厳島神社]]の奥宮である。 ==祭神== 厳島神社同様、[[宗像三女神]]を祀る。 *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） ==歴史== [[平清盛]]が厳島神社を造営した際、奥宮として建立したと伝えられる。
[[江戸時代]]までは[[三鬼大権現|三鬼神]]（追帳鬼神・魔羅鬼神・時眉鬼神）を祀る三鬼堂であったが、 [[神仏分離令]]により宗像三女神を祀る御山神社となり、三鬼堂は[[弥山本堂]]近くに新たに建立された。 ==社殿・境内== 三女神を祀る[[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿三棟が品字形に並ぶ。 ==交通アクセス== * [[広島観光開発|宮島ロープウェー]]獅子岩駅から、弥山本堂経由で約30分。 ==関連項目== *[[厳島神社]] *[[弥山 (広島県)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやましんしや}} [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]] [[Category:廿日市市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Created page with "== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊). == Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within..."
revision.text:
== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 御山神社 |画像= [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
社殿 |所在地= 広島県廿日市市宮島町 |緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =16 |緯度秒 =39.62 |経度度 =132 |経度分 =19 |経度秒 =6.71 |地図= Japan Hiroshima |祭神= 市杵島姫命、田心姫命、湍津姫命 |社格= |創建= |本殿= 一間社流造 |別名= さんきさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''御山神社'''（みやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]宮島町にある[[神社]]。[[厳島神社]]奥宮で、[[厳島|宮島]]（厳島）の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]山頂付近に所在する。 ==祭神== 厳島神社同様、[[宗像三女神]]を祀る。 *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） ==歴史== [[平清盛]]が厳島神社を造営した際、奥宮として建立したと伝えられる。 [[江戸時代]]までは[[三鬼大権現|三鬼神]]（追帳鬼神・魔羅鬼神・時眉鬼神）を祀る三鬼堂であったが、[[神仏分離令]]により宗像三女神を祀る御山神社となり、三鬼堂は[[弥山本堂]]近くに新たに建立された。 == 境内 == 三女神を祀る[[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿三棟が品字形に並ぶ。 ==交通アクセス== * [[広島観光開発|宮島ロープウェー]]獅子岩駅から、弥山本堂経由で約30分。 ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} *[[厳島神社]] *[[弥山 (広島県)]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]] [[Category:廿日市市の建築物]] [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

{{神社 |名称= 御山神社 |画像= [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
社殿 |所在地= 広島県廿日市市宮島町 |緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =16 |緯度秒 =39.62 |経度度 =132 |経度分 =19 |経度秒 =6.71 |地図= Japan Hiroshima |祭神= 市杵島姫命、田心姫命、湍津姫命 |社格= |創建= |本殿= 一間社流造 |別名= さんきさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''御山神社'''（みやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]宮島町にある[[神社]]。[[厳島神社]]奥宮で、[[厳島|宮島]]（厳島）の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]山頂付近に所在する。 ==祭神== 厳島神社同様、[[宗像三女神]]を祀る。 *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） ==歴史== [[平清盛]]が厳島神社を造営した際、奥宮として建立したと伝えられる。 [[江戸時代]]までは[[三鬼大権現|三鬼神]]（追帳鬼神・魔羅鬼神・時眉鬼神）を祀る三鬼堂であったが、[[神仏分離令]]により宗像三女神を祀る御山神社となり、三鬼堂は[[弥山本堂]]近くに新たに建立された。 == 境内 == 三女神を祀る[[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿三棟が品字形に並ぶ。 ==交通アクセス== * [[広島観光開発|宮島ロープウェー]]獅子岩駅から、弥山本堂経由で約30分。 ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} *[[厳島神社]] *[[弥山 (広島県)]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]] [[Category:廿日市市の建築物]] [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

{{Shrine | Name = Miyama Shrine | Image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | Location = Miyajima-cho, [[Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | Deity = [[Ichikishimahime]], [[Tagorihime]], [[Tagitsuhime]] | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, Miyama Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] (Itsukushima) in [[Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the **Okumiya (inner shrine) of [[Itsukushima Shrine]]** and is situated near the **summit of Mount Misen**. ## Deities Like **Itsukushima Shrine**, Miyama Shrine enshrines the **three Munakata goddesses** (宗像三女神): * **Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto** (市杵島姫命) * **Tagorihime-no-Mikoto** (田心姫命) * **Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto** (湍津姫命) ## History According to tradition, **Taira no Kiyomori** constructed the shrine as the **Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine**. Until the **Edo period**, the shrine was known as the **Sanki-dō (三鬼堂)** and enshrined the **Three Demon Gods (Sanki Shin)**: * **Suichō-oni Shin** (追帳鬼神) * **Mara-oni Shin** (魔羅鬼神) * **Jibi-oni Shin** (時眉鬼神) However, after the **Shinbutsu Bunri (Separation of Shinto and Buddhism)** decree during the **Meiji era**, the Sanki-dō was renamed **Miyama Shrine**, enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The **Sanki-dō** itself was relocated and rebuilt near the **Misen Main Hall (弥山本堂)**. ## Shrine Grounds The shrine features **three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls**, arranged in a **品 (hin) shape** to enshrine the three goddesses. ## Access * **From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station**, it is about a **30-minute hike** via the **Misen Main Hall**. ## Related Articles {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

{{Shrine | Name = Miyama Shrine | Image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | Location = Miyajima-cho, [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | Deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ({{ill|Itsukushima|ja|厳島}}) in [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the **Okumiya** (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and is situated near the **summit of Mount Misen**. ## Deities Like **Itsukushima Shrine**, Miyama Shrine enshrines the **Three Munakata Goddesses** ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}): * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} ## History According to tradition, **Taira no Kiyomori** ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}) constructed the shrine as the **Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine**. Until the **Edo period**, the shrine was known as the **Sanki-dō** (三鬼堂) and enshrined the **Three Demon Gods** (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * **Suichō-oni Shin** (追帳鬼神) * **Mara-oni Shin** (魔羅鬼神) * **Jibi-oni Shin** (時眉鬼神) However, after the **Shinbutsu Bunri** ({{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}) decree during the **Meiji era**, the Sanki-dō was renamed **Miyama Shrine**, enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The **Sanki-dō** itself was relocated and rebuilt near the **Misen Main Hall** ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). ## Shrine Grounds The shrine features **three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls**, arranged in a **品 (hin) shape** to enshrine the three goddesses. ## Access * **From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station**, it is about a **30-minute hike** via the **Misen Main Hall**. ## Related Articles {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

The **Sanki-dō** itself was relocated and rebuilt near the **Misen Main Hall** ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). ## Shrine Grounds The shrine features **three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls**, arranged in a **品 (hin) shape** to enshrine the three goddesses. ## Access * **From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station**, it is about a **30-minute hike** via the **Misen Main Hall**. ## Related Articles {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed:Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. ## Deities Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}): * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} ## History According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). ## Shrine Grounds The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. ## Access * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. ## Related Articles {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed:Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:new pages]]

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). ## Shrine Grounds The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. ## Access * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. ## Related Articles {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed|Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qqq]]

Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ({{ill|Itsukushima|ja|厳島}}) in [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. ## Deities Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}): * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} ## History According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). ## Shrine Grounds The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. ## Access * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. ## Related Articles {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed|Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[sn:iyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed|Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:御山神社]][[Category:qq]][[sn:iyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}): * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}): * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed|Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[ja:御山神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:iyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ({{ill|Itsukushima|ja|厳島}}) in [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}): * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * {{ill|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{citation needed|Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[ja:御山神社]] [[ja:御山神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:iyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyama Shrine}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miyama Shrine]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 御山神社 |画像= [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
社殿 |所在地= 広島県廿日市市宮島町 |緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =16 |緯度秒 =39.62 |経度度 =132 |経度分 =19 |経度秒 =6.71 |地図= Japan Hiroshima |祭神= 市杵島姫命、田心姫命、湍津姫命 |社格= |創建= |本殿= 一間社流造 |別名= さんきさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''御山神社'''（みやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]宮島町にある[[神社]]。[[厳島神社]]奥宮で、[[厳島|宮島]]（厳島）の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]山頂付近に所在する。 ==祭神== 厳島神社同様、[[宗像三女神]]を祀る。 *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） ==歴史== [[平清盛]]が厳島神社を造営した際、奥宮として建立したと伝えられる。 [[江戸時代]]までは[[三鬼大権現|三鬼神]]（追帳鬼神・魔羅鬼神・時眉鬼神）を祀る三鬼堂であったが、[[神仏分離令]]により宗像三女神を祀る御山神社となり、三鬼堂は[[弥山本堂]]近くに新たに建立された。 == 境内 == 三女神を祀る[[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿三棟が品字形に並ぶ。 ==交通アクセス== * [[広島観光開発|宮島ロープウェー]]獅子岩駅から、弥山本堂経由で約30分。 ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} *[[厳島神社]] *[[弥山 (広島県)]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]] [[Category:廿日市市の建築物]] [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 御山神社 |画像= [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
社殿 |所在地= 広島県廿日市市宮島町 |緯度度 =34 |緯度分 =16 |緯度秒 =39.62 |経度度 =132 |経度分 =19 |経度秒 =6.71 |地図= Japan Hiroshima |祭神= 市杵島姫命、田心姫命、湍津姫命 |社格= |創建= |本殿= 一間社流造 |別名= さんきさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''御山神社'''（みやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]宮島町にある[[神社]]。[[厳島神社]]奥宮で、[[厳島|宮島]]（厳島）の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]山頂付近に所在する。 ==祭神== 厳島神社同様、[[宗像三女神]]を祀る。 *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いちきしまひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） ==歴史== [[平清盛]]が厳島神社を造営した際、奥宮として建立したと伝えられる。 [[江戸時代]]までは[[三鬼大権現|三鬼神]]（追帳鬼神・魔羅鬼神・時眉鬼神）を祀る三鬼堂であったが、[[神仏分離令]]により宗像三女神を祀る御山神社となり、三鬼堂は[[弥山本堂]]近くに新たに建立された。 == 境内 == 三女神を祀る[[流造|一間社流造]]の本殿三棟が品字形に並ぶ。 ==交通アクセス== * [[広島観光開発|宮島ロープウェー]]獅子岩駅から、弥山本堂経由で約30分。 ==関連項目== {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} *[[厳島神社]] *[[弥山 (広島県)]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]] [[Category:廿日市市の建築物]] [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:iyama Shrine]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]]

It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:iyama Shrine]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]] [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]]

Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:iyama Shrine]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]] [[Category:厳島神社 (広島県)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]]

Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|Hatsukaichi]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ({{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:iyama Shrine]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]]

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:iyama Shrine]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:廿日市市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:iyama Shrine]] [[sn:Miyama Shrine]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}}

It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17215812]] Wikidata name: Miyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17215812]] Wikidata name: Miyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. [[sn:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17215812]] Wikidata name: Miyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q17215812}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17215812}}

[[commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17215812]] Wikidata name: Miyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17215812}}

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17215812]] Wikidata name: Miyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]
