The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17215812]] Wikidata name: Miyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on [[Miyajima]] ([[:en:Itsukushima|Itsukushima]]) in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]]) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen (Hiroshima)]] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located on [[Miyajima]] ({{ill|Itsukushima|en|Itsukushima|WD=Q875301|lt=Itsukushima}}) in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen (Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen (Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen (Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen (Hiroshima)}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Miyama-jinja]][[ja:御山神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Miyama Shrine | image = [[File:Miyama-jinja (Hatsukaichi), shaden-1.jpg|280px]]
Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi|1=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on [[Miyajima]] ({{ill|Itsukushima|en|Itsukushima|WD=Q875301|lt=Itsukushima|1=Itsukushima}}) in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi|1=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|1=Mount Misen}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Shrine Hall | location = Miyajima-cho, {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi|1=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34|16|39.62|N|132|19|6.71|E|type:landmark_region:JP-34}} | Map = Japan Hiroshima | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} | Status = | Established = | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri|Single-bay Nagare-zukuri]] | Other Names = Sankisan | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Miyama Shrine''' (御山神社, ''Miyama Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on [[Miyajima]] ({{ill|Itsukushima|en|Itsukushima|WD=Q875301|lt=Itsukushima|1=Itsukushima}}) in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi|1=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses. The '''Sanki-dō''' itself was relocated and rebuilt near the '''Misen Main Hall''' ({{ill|Misen Hondo|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Hondo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Shrine Grounds == The shrine features '''three single-bay Nagare-zukuri halls''', arranged in a '''品 (hin) shape''' to enshrine the three goddesses. == Access == * '''From Miyajima Ropeway Shishiiwa Station''', it is about a '''30-minute hike''' via the '''Misen Main Hall'''. == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Miyama-jinja}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen (Hiroshima)|1=Mount Misen}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|御山神社|version=89488036|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御山神社]] {{Commons category|Miyama-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17215812}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Itsukushima Shinto Shrine]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hatsukaichi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It serves as the '''Okumiya''' (inner shrine) of {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} and is situated near the '''summit of Mount Misen'''. == Deities == Like '''Itsukushima Shrine''', Miyama Shrine enshrines the '''Three Munakata Goddesses''' ({{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}): * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} == History == According to tradition, '''Taira no Kiyomori''' ([[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}) constructed the shrine as the '''Okumiya (inner shrine) of Itsukushima Shrine'''. Until the '''Edo period''', the shrine was known as the '''Sanki-dō''' (三鬼堂) and enshrined the '''[[Sanki Daigongen|Three Demon Gods]]''' (三鬼神, ''Sanki Shin''): * '''Suichō-oni Shin''' (追帳鬼神) * '''Mara-oni Shin''' (魔羅鬼神) * '''Jibi-oni Shin''' (時眉鬼神) However, after the '''Shinbutsu Bunri''' ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}) decree during the '''Meiji era''', the Sanki-dō was renamed '''Miyama Shrine''', enshrining the Munakata goddesses.
